code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: ONLY a single number: 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5
    - Do NOT output any explanation, text, symbols, or punctuation
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
    - Explicit repurchasing/gifting behavior mentioned despite minor issues →5
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Safety hazards/complete failure with anger/return statements
    - Multiple unresolved issues with warnings against purchase
    - Updates negating initial satisfaction →1
    - Explicit quality condemnations ("junk", "scam")

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Functional but with major compromises affecting regular use
    - Short lifespan/degradation despite proper maintenance
    - Worse than cheaper alternatives requiring workarounds →2
    - Only performs superficial functions while failing core tasks →2

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with neutral/conflicted language
    - Satisfies basic needs with significant compromises
    - Temporary solutions with partial effectiveness →3

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Specific minor flaws with maintenance requirements →4
    - "Good but not best" comparisons with clear limitations
    - Style/design praise with functional compromises →4

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Core excellence outweighs minor issues
    - Exceeds skeptical expectations through transformative utility →5
    - Long-term satisfaction with accepted tradeoffs →5
    - Niche excellence justifying inherent requirements →5

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - Maintenance with reluctant acceptance →4 vs enthusiastic acceptance →5
    - "Works best when..." optimizations →5 vs "Avoid..." constraints →4
    - Price comparisons with maintained functionality →5
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "UPDATE: Replacement failed in 2 months. These models are junk!"
<Reasoning>Multiple rapid failures with explicit quality condemnation</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Only freshens carpets, never deep cleans. Worse than cheaper models."
<Reasoning>Fails core task while performing superficially</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Adequate for light use but bends under heavy items."
<Reasoning>Mixed functionality with clear limitations</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Superior grind quality but requires weekly calibration. Deducted a star."
<Reasoning>Explicit deduction for maintenance requirement</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Solved my decade-old cleaning problem despite noisy operation!"
<Reasoning>Transformative utility outweighs operational flaw</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "These napkins stay crisp but need ironing after every wash."
<Reasoning>Core functionality with high maintenance cost</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Between puppy messes and kids, this cleaner keeps surprising me!"
<Reasoning>Exceeded skeptical expectations through core effectiveness</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Compact design rusted quickly. Looks great but flawed."
<Reasoning>Style praise with functional failure</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "UPDATE: 1 year later, still works perfectly despite occasional noise."
<Reasoning>Long-term satisfaction with accepted tradeoff</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (37 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 14
  gold 5 -> 4: 13
  gold 4 -> 3: 2
  gold 1 -> 2: 2
  gold 1 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 3, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "They're okay, but not extraordinary. They seem to be well made and are holding up so far with no problems, and the fact that you can toss them into the washing machine to be cleaned is nice. They're just a bit on the thin side as well, though, so you..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 3, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I can forgive the fact that there was blood on one of the math mats when I opened the package.  Things happen and I'm sorry that someone was injured.  What I can't get past is the fact that there are so many snags.  It's like these two mats were seco..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [2, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "Just got this in and so far, so good. It is lightweight and picks up the sand on my porcelain tile. Not many vacuums can get the sand from this tile and the grout and so far, this one has done the job.
UPDATE!!!! In April, the wheel came off and ther..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Returned. Only because I decided to go with tiles and not a rug"
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I've never tried an air purifier before, though I know people who have/had them. This one was not impressive. First, it has an odor. I don't know if it's because of impurities that emit a smell once trapped in the gadget and exposed to heat from the ..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [2, 2, 1] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I hate to review these towels as 3 stars, but the particular set I have (Skeleton Applique) is just that. Actually they are more of a 2 star set, but I'll explain my 3 star rating later on.

The gold and black striped towels are not absorbent at all ..."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [2, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 37 errors, 15 borderline out of 80 examples."}
